consumes.artifact: specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml (when a prior spec exists, for completion gate check per fr-rf-012)

requirement.title: Block on Design Contradictions (R3)
requirement.description: Refiner MUST block when the design doc contains contradictory requirements or when the design doc's Change Intent contradicts the prior spec, per Rule R3 (Block on Contradictions). Blocks are transient: refiner prints a detailed error message to terminal and exits non-zero. No artifact is written on block — no spec.xml, no details/, no report file. Retry semantics are clean: fix the design doc, re-run refiner.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a design doc where REQ-A requires "sessions expire after 15 minutes" and REQ-B requires "sessions never expire", refiner MUST print the contradiction to terminal with specific requirement IDs and exit non-zero. REQ-F008
When blocked, refiner MUST NOT write or modify any file — not spec.xml, not details/, not any report file. Nothing persists across the blocked invocation. REQ-F008
Refiner MUST NOT write any refiner-report.md or equivalent persistent artifact on block. The only output channels on block are terminal (stderr) and the process exit code. REQ-F009

Terminal output on block MUST list each detected issue (contradiction, missing acceptance criterion, broken dependency) with the specific requirement ID(s) involved, and MUST include a Recommendations section with a concrete suggestion for resolving each listed issue. REQ-F009

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header
requirement.description: Every spec.xml MUST contain an identity header with spec_id, spec_version, status, source (design path + iteration date), scope, and supersedes fields.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A newly created spec.xml MUST have spec_version: 1, status: active, and supersedes empty or absent. REQ-F010
An updated spec.xml MUST have spec_version: base_version + 1, and supersedes set to "<spec-id>:v<previous-version>". REQ-F010

The source element MUST contain design_path pointing to the exact design doc file and design_iteration matching the design doc folder name. The folder name MUST start with an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) and MAY include a "-"-separated human-chosen suffix (e.g., "2026-04-16-v2" for same-date sequential iterations, "2026-04-16-oauth-pivot" for intent-tagged iterations). The suffix is a human convention, not a schema constraint. REQ-F010
The scope element MUST list included features with IDs and excluded items with reasons. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Per-Spec Directory Isolation
requirement.description: Multiple specs MUST coexist in separate specs/<spec-id>/ folders. Each folder is a self-contained project unit.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given spec-ids "auth" and "payments", their specs MUST reside at specs/auth/spec.xml and specs/payments/spec.xml respectively, with no cross-folder references. REQ-F011
Each spec folder MUST contain its own details/ subdirectory. Detail files MUST NOT reference requirements from other spec folders. REQ-F011

requirement.title: Formalize Spec From Design Doc
requirement.description: Refiner MUST read the complete design doc and produce or update a spec. There is no explicit mode distinction — the refiner checks whether specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists and adapts accordingly. When no existing spec is found, it formalizes from scratch. When an existing spec is found, it reads the design doc (Change Intent) alongside the existing spec, determines what changed, and updates the spec — like wiki ingest propagation.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When no existing spec.xml is found, refiner MUST produce a spec.xml with spec_version: 1 and detail files covering all requirements identified in the design doc, each with at least one acceptance criterion and trace. REQ-F012

When an existing spec.xml is found, refiner MUST read both the design doc and the existing spec. ADDED items result in new requirements; MODIFIED items update existing requirements in place; REMOVED items delete requirements from detail files. REQ-F013
When updating an existing spec, requirements NOT affected by the design doc's change intent MUST remain unchanged in the output spec. REQ-F013

A complete rewrite (aggressive iteration where the Change Intent replaces everything) MUST be handled by the same behavior — the refiner does not switch to a "replace" code path. The design doc's Change Intent drives what gets added/modified/removed/renamed; the refiner emits a correspondingly large <delta>. No mode labels, no separate behavior — just one refiner that produces whatever delta the situation requires. REQ-F012, REQ-F013

requirement.title: Delta Metadata Output — Wiki-Style Accumulation
requirement.description: When updating an existing spec, refiner MUST append a new <delta> element to the spec's <overview> recording what changed in this version. Refiner MUST NOT overwrite, modify, or remove prior <delta> elements — they are historical record. <overview> accumulates all <delta> children ordered ascending by version, producing a self-contained iteration history inside the spec (wiki-style, per Karpathy three-layer model). This metadata stays within the spec (wiki layer) so the planner can determine planning scope without reading the design doc (raw source layer).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A new <delta> element MUST include: version (matching the new spec_version), iteration (matching the new design_iteration), and child elements for each change. Supported child elements are <added ref="req-id" />, <modified ref="req-id" />, <removed ref="req-id"><reason>...</reason></removed> (reason required; optional <migration> child), and <renamed ref_old="..." ref_new="..."> (optional <reason>; body unchanged — semantic changes use removed+added, not renamed). REQ-F013
For a new spec (no prior version), no delta element is written. Absence of any <delta> in <overview> signals "plan all requirements" to the planner. REQ-F012

Every requirement ID referenced in the new delta MUST correspond to an actual requirement in the spec's detail files (for added/modified/ renamed.ref_new) or to a requirement that existed in a prior version (for removed.ref and renamed.ref_old). REQ-F013

Refiner MUST preserve all prior <delta> elements verbatim when writing the new spec. The new <delta> is appended as the last child of <overview>. The resulting sequence MUST be ordered ascending by version. Refiner MUST NOT merge, deduplicate, or rewrite historical deltas. REQ-F013
The new delta's version attribute MUST equal the new spec_version. The new delta's iteration attribute MUST equal the new design_iteration. Mismatch is ERROR. REQ-F013

requirement.title: DAG Completion Gate (Upstream of New Iteration)
requirement.description: Before producing a new spec version (one that would append a <delta> and increment spec_version), refiner MUST check the current specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml for sprint completion. This gate lives in refiner, not planner, so that inconsistent wiki-layer state ("spec at v(N+1) while prior sprint's DAG is still incomplete") never arises. If the gate blocks, nothing downstream moves — spec stays at v(N), DAG stays at v(N)'s execution state, user completes the work or abandons the entire spec, then the refiner may run cleanly.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When no prior spec.xml exists, refiner MUST NOT perform the gate check (there is no prior sprint to be incomplete). Refiner proceeds as v1 formalization. REQ-F023
When a prior spec.xml exists AND no dag.yaml exists at specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml, refiner MUST proceed. This is a legal state (user refined without planning yet). REQ-F023
When a prior spec.xml exists AND dag.yaml exists AND all epics in dag.yaml are in "completed" status, refiner MUST proceed with the new iteration. REQ-F023

When a prior spec.xml exists AND dag.yaml exists AND any epic is NOT in "completed" status, refiner MUST BLOCK. It MUST print to terminal: the number of incomplete epics, the specific epic IDs and their statuses, and the message "Complete current sprint before iterating." Refiner MUST exit non-zero. No spec file is written, no <delta> is added, nothing changes on disk. No refiner-report.md is written. REQ-F023

Refiner MUST NOT implement any escape hatch for the gate — no --force flag, no environment variable override, no "abandoned" epic status that bypasses the check. The only legitimate paths forward when blocked are: (a) the user completes the remaining epics, or (b) the user abandons the entire spec by deleting specs/<spec-id>/ (which is a filesystem action outside refiner's scope). Partial abandonment is rejected by design because it would pollute dag.yaml status semantics. REQ-F023

requirement.title: Spec Version Increment
requirement.description: When updating an existing spec, refiner MUST increment the spec version and record supersedes. The planner uses spec_version to locate the correct delta for planning scope.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Updated spec.xml MUST have spec_version = previous version + 1. REQ-F010
Updated spec.xml MUST set supersedes to "<spec-id>:v<previous-version>". REQ-F010
Updated spec.xml source MUST point to the new design doc iteration that triggered this update. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Merged Spec Validation
requirement.description: After producing a new or updated spec, refiner MUST validate that the resulting spec has no internal contradictions before writing output.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given an update that adds a requirement contradicting an existing requirement, refiner MUST block per fr-rf-001 (terminal output, non-zero exit, no files written). The contradiction MUST be described in terminal output with specific requirement IDs. REQ-F015
Given an update that modifies a requirement into contradiction with another requirement, refiner MUST block. REQ-F015
Given an update that removes a requirement depended on by another requirement still in the spec, refiner MUST block with a "dependency broken" issue. REQ-F015

requirement.title: Input Validation — Design Doc
requirement.description: Before any formalization, refiner MUST validate its input per the design doc contract (fr-cc-if-001). All validation checks run before any output is produced. The refiner consumes the complete design doc.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Refiner MUST verify the design doc file exists at the provided path. Missing file is ERROR with remediation: "Run brainstorming to produce a design doc first." REQ-F008

Refiner MUST check filesystem state to decide whether version-dependent fields are produced: if specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists → read existing spec + design doc, produce updated spec with incremented spec_version, supersedes, and a new <delta>. If not → read design doc, produce new spec with spec_version=1, no supersedes, no delta. This is a single behavior with conditional fields, not two modes. REQ-F012, REQ-F013

When a prior spec exists, refiner MUST validate that the design doc has the required sections for an iteration design doc: Context section (with spec version reference) and Change Intent section present. Missing required sections is ERROR with remediation: "When a prior spec exists, the design doc must include Context and Change Intent sections — re-run brainstorming with the prior spec in scope." REQ-F005, REQ-F008

When a prior spec exists, if the design doc's date folder is older than or equal to the spec's recorded design_iteration, refiner MUST produce WARNING: "design iteration <date> is not newer than spec source <spec-date> — this may be a stale design doc." REQ-F026

requirement.title: Output Validation — Two-Pass Write
requirement.description: Refiner MUST follow the two-pass write pattern (fr-cc-val-001): build the complete spec.xml and details/ in memory, validate against the Spec Identity Header contract (fr-cc-if-002), then write to disk only if validation passes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After building spec.xml in memory, refiner MUST validate the identity header per fr-cc-if-002: spec_id, spec_version, status, source, scope all present and well-formed. Missing fields are ERROR. REQ-F010, REQ-F015
Every <requirement> in the built output MUST have at least one <acceptance_criteria> with a <trace> element. Requirements without testable AC are ERROR. REQ-F012
If output validation finds ERRORs, refiner MUST report all findings with remediation guidance and MUST NOT write any files to specs/<spec-id>/. REQ-F008, REQ-F015

If output validation passes (zero ERRORs), refiner MUST write all files atomically — spec.xml and all details/*.xml in a single operation. Partial writes (spec.xml written but details/ incomplete) MUST NOT occur. REQ-F012
